description: Split the audio signal into adjustable fixed length chunks

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph. No effect on audio processing.

Selects the fixed block size for sub-block chunking. Valid values: 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512. Stored as a string NodeProperty. At C++ export time, the current value is baked in as a compile-time constant (equivalent to using the corresponding fixN_block node). Hidden by default in the UI -- must be shown via the parameter button.

gaps.question: How does FixedBlockXNode dispatch to the correct fix_block<N> template at runtime? The infrastructure doc mentions a switch statement. Does each block size option instantiate a separate compiled code path, or is there a single dynamic path?
gaps.context: Affects understanding of runtime overhead vs the fixN_block nodes. The existing phase3 doc says compilation removes the branching overhead.

gaps.question: What happens if the BlockSize property is set to an invalid value (e.g., not a power of two, or a value like 4 that is below the HISE_EVENT_RASTER)? The infrastructure doc says 'defaults to 64 if invalid'. What constitutes invalid?
gaps.context: Users editing the property need to know what values are accepted and what happens with out-of-range values.

gaps.question: Does fix_blockx have the same bypass behaviour as fixN_block (children still process with original block size when bypassed)?
gaps.context: The existing phase3 doc does not mention bypass. Need to confirm if the A/B workflow from fix8_block applies here too.

gaps.question: The existing doc says compilation bakes the current BlockSize property into a static value. Is this done by emitting a fix_block<N> template instantiation in the C++ export, effectively removing the switch dispatch?
gaps.context: Important for users deciding between fix_blockx (development flexibility) and fixN_block (no runtime overhead even before compilation).

notes: Related to the fixN_block variant group but not itself a variant -- it is the dynamic-property version. Uses wrap::fix_blockx<T, DynamicBlockProperty> internally with a switch statement dispatching to fix_block<N> for each valid block size. The existing phase3 doc has good content about the development workflow (evaluate block sizes, then compile). The BlockSize property is hidden by default, which is a UX detail worth preserving in the reference page.
